newsSince: No material company-specific news since June 9. ADI's record Q2 FY26 (revenue +37% y/y, data-center comms +90%), the raised Q3 guide, and the closed $1.5B Empower Semiconductor acquisition all predate June 9 and were already in the prior verdict's tape. The ~+1% move since June 9 is benign.

sources.label: Futurum: ADI Q2 FY26 AI data-center/power demand
Simply Wall St: ADI buys Empower for bigger AI-power role

verdicts.rankRationale: Residual-alpha-vs-passives lens: $200B mega-cap, +49% YTD, Buy consensus and post-earnings/Empower rerating already in the tape leave little hidden alpha. No new news since June 9. Hold at #40 in the secondary residual bucket.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Mega-cap with consensus already long; residual alpha largely captured
verdicts.bull: Empower closes the AI vertical-power gap; data-center comms +90% gives a real, durable AI tailwind on a high-quality analog franchise.
verdicts.neutral: Great franchise but $200B cap and +49% YTD mean the AI-power story is well-owned, not mispriced.
verdicts.bear: Little residual alpha left after the rerating; industrial-cycle exposure caps upside surprise.

verdicts.rankRationale: AI-node-bottleneck lens: analog/power/RF is selective, less-direct AI-node exposure than HBM, advanced packaging or AI networking. No new node-relevant news since June 9; prior down-move already priced the crowding. Hold at #71.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Vertical/intermediate-bus power adoption for AI compute racks
verdicts.primaryRisk: Not a core AI-node bottleneck; analog content trails HBM/packaging/networking
verdicts.bull: 48/54V intermediate-bus modules and first vertical-power customer give incremental AI-node power content.
verdicts.neutral: AI-adjacent but not a primary node chokepoint; ranks appropriately in the lower analog tier.
verdicts.bear: AI exposure is indirect; demand is partly industrial-cycle, not pure node bottleneck.

verdicts.rankRationale: Co-packaged-optics lens: a quality analog/power franchise with no early CPO exposure. No CPO-relevant news since June 9; prior cut already reflected the no-CPO argument. Hold at #96.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: None CPO-specific; power-management leverage to optical-heavy AI racks is indirect
verdicts.primaryRisk: No early CPO content; ranked low on this lens by design
verdicts.bull: Power/signal-chain content sits adjacent to optical AI infrastructure, an indirect CPO read-through.
verdicts.neutral: Solid franchise but essentially zero direct co-packaged-optics exposure for this lens.
verdicts.bear: Irrelevant to the CPO transition; belongs in the analog/power tail here.
